name: feedback_no_blanket_count_labels
purpose: Prohibit using frequency-based terms like 'singleton' as category labels when discussing domain content — describe substance, not count.
notes: T40 vocabulary seeding context. No project-specific refs. Fully portable.
portable_elements: Universal AI-communication principle: describe substance not frequency counts when categorizing items.

name: feedback_no_broken_widgets
purpose: Dashboard tabs must ship complete with full functionality — no placeholder states or minimum-viable stubs.
sanitize_fields: purpose — dashboard/command-center framing is SoNash-specific; portable as 'deliverables must ship complete'
notes: No frontmatter originSessionId. Dashboard/tab framing is SoNash-specific but principle (don't ship stubs) is universal. Sanitize framing for JASON-OS.
portable_elements: Ship complete or not at all. Fix data gaps before building features. Never rationalize stubs as acceptable.

name: feedback_no_incomplete_agent_findings
purpose: Agent results that skip, sample, or leave gaps are unacceptable — dispatch fill-in agents until every item has individual structured verification.
notes: Session #264 origin. Contains specific PR numbers (#492, #489, #487) as examples — these are SoNash history but the principle is fully portable. Already present in JASON-OS CLAUDE.md §4 guardrail 15.
portable_elements: Never silently accept partial agent data. Gap found → dispatch fill-in. Default stance is completeness over speed.

name: feedback_no_preexisting_rejection
purpose: Pre-existing is not a valid rejection reason in PR reviews — always present fix-or-DEBT options to the user.
notes: CONTENT-BLEED: canonical version reformats frontmatter description to multi-line, removes originSessionId field. User-home version has single-line description and no status field in original — both now have status:active. User-home body adds 'This applies regardless of how many codebase-wide instances exist.' Canonical is slightly more terse. Substantively equivalent. Has canonical in sonash-v0.
portable_elements: Pre-existing is not a rejection reason. Always offer fix-now or DEBT-item options. Applies universally to any review process.

name: feedback_no_premature_next_steps
purpose: Do not jump to next phase without being explicitly asked — user controls workflow progression.
notes: Session #244 origin. Extended rule set including session-end assumptions and 'anything else?' handling. Already in JASON-OS CLAUDE.md and MEMORY.md. Canonical gap: no canonical equivalent in sonash-v0.
portable_elements: After completing research/plan/execution, present results and wait. 'Proceed' means current thing. Never propose session-end as default action.

name: feedback_no_silent_deferrals_execution
purpose: Never mark a plan step done while silently skipping sub-items — flag any uncompleted deliverables immediately.
notes: T28 Session #269 origin (7 silently deferred items). No project-specific refs beyond T28 context example. Fully portable principle.
portable_elements: Before marking step done, compare against 'Done when' criteria. Missing deliverable = immediate flag. Never rationalize skipping planned items.

name: feedback_no_silent_skill_failures
purpose: Skills must detect, retry with mitigation, and report failures — never silently skip steps.
notes: Triggered by repo-analysis skipping repomix generation. Pattern generalizes: verify expected output after each SHOULD/MUST step. Already in JASON-OS CLAUDE.md §4 guardrail 9 and MEMORY.md.
portable_elements: After every SHOULD/MUST step, verify output exists. If missing: retry with mitigation. If retry fails: report options, never silently continue.

name: feedback_no_stale_ci_assumption
purpose: Never assume CI failures are stale runs — verify commit SHA in CI logs before dismissing.
notes: PR #503 R3 origin. Already in JASON-OS MEMORY.md. No project-specific content beyond example PR reference.
portable_elements: Verify commit SHA in CI logs matches branch HEAD before rejecting failure as stale.

name: feedback_no_unnecessary_brainstorming
purpose: Do not invoke /brainstorm when the user has already decided what to do — brainstorming is for exploration, not execution of decided work.
notes: Session #265 (2026-04-06) origin — user asked to plan re-running repo-analysis on 6 repos. Dates present but principle is fully portable. Already in JASON-OS MEMORY.md.
portable_elements: If user says 'lets do X' with clear parameters, just do it. Brainstorm only for 'what should approach be?', not 'do this concrete task'.

name: feedback_permissions_cleanup
purpose: User prefers tiered permissions: clean project-level wildcards, near-empty settings.local.json, cross-project patterns in global settings.
sanitize_fields: PR numbers and session references (PR #469, Session #238, 2026-03-25 dates)
notes: Tiered permission model (project settings.json / settings.local.json / global) is universally applicable but the specific counts/PR/date references are SoNash-specific. Sanitize those for JASON-OS.
portable_elements: Tiered permissions model. Never let settings.local.json accumulate past ~10 entries without suggesting cleanup.

name: feedback_pr_review_state_files
purpose: pr-review skill must persist round counts in state files so compaction/clear doesn't lose cross-round data; state files must also be git-committed for cross-locale portability.
notes: CONTENT-BLEED: canonical version is MISSING the cross-locale git-commit requirement ('state files must be git committed along with each review round's fix commit'). User-home adds: 'User works across locales/machines — if state files aren't committed, switching locales loses all prior-round context (as happened with PR #453 R3 on 2026-03-19).' Canonical is an older, less complete version. This is a real divergence — canonical needs updating.
portable_elements: State data must be in state files not conversation memory. State files must survive compaction. For cross-locale work, state files must be git-committed.

name: feedback_precommit_fixer_report
purpose: After fixing pre-commit failures, present the structured PRE-COMMIT FIX RESULT report and wait for user confirmation before re-committing.
notes: Contains the exact report format template inline. Already in JASON-OS MEMORY.md. Fully portable.
portable_elements: Structured PRE-COMMIT FIX RESULT report format. Always present before re-committing. User retains approval control.

name: feedback_repo_analysis_knowledge_gap
purpose: Repo analysis must extract knowledge candidates alongside pattern candidates — Creator View mandatory, extraction tracking mandatory (v4.1 resolution history).
sanitize_fields: version numbers (v3.0/v4.0/v4.1) are SoNash repo-analysis specific
notes: Description says 'FULLY ADDRESSED in v4.1' — status is historical reference. Contains resolution timeline (v3.0 2026-04-03, v4.0/v4.1 2026-04-05). Core principle (knowledge extraction > pattern extraction) is portable but implementation details are SoNash-specific to repo-analysis skill.
portable_elements: Knowledge extraction is the most important dimension of analysis. Value-map should have both pattern and knowledge candidates. Watch for regression toward purely clinical/technical extraction.

name: feedback_repo_analysis_phase_ordering
purpose: Multiple phase ordering and completeness errors in repo-analysis: Phase 4b before 4, Phase 6c before self-audit, coverage audit is interactive, EXTRACTIONS.md in self-audit checklist.
sanitize_fields: Phase numbers (4b, 4, 6c, 6b) are repo-analysis SKILL.md specific
notes: Session #276 ArchiveBox analysis origin. Phase ordering applies to SoNash repo-analysis skill specifically. General principle (phase ordering matters, interactive gates must not be silently skipped) is portable.
portable_elements: Phase ordering is structural, not just informational — enforce sequence. Interactive gates must always be presented, never auto-decided. Self-audit checklist must verify actual artifact presence, not assumed completion.

name: feedback_routing_menu_value
purpose: User enjoys routing menu options in repo-analysis — external routing discovery (TDMS/deep-plan/cross-repo synthesis) adds value and should not be simplified.
sanitize_fields: TDMS and specific routing destinations are SoNash-specific skill names
notes: SoNash-specific to repo-analysis routing menu. General principle: breadth of routing options to downstream workflows is valuable — don't simplify routing menus in skills. Portable if skill names replaced.
portable_elements: Post-analysis routing menus that surface non-obvious next actions add user value. Keep routing broad, not minimal.

name: feedback_scope_drift_deep_research
purpose: Deep-research must be scoped on the user's actual goal, not on a supporting technical sub-component — T28 lost a full session to data-layer scope drift.
notes: T28 Session #267 'Intelligence Graph Data Layer' scope-drift story. Already in JASON-OS MEMORY.md. Specific session/PR refs are SoNash context but principle is fully portable and critical.
portable_elements: Before scoping deep-research, restate user's original goal and confirm research maps to it. Infrastructure research only AFTER feature design is settled. Watch for brainstorm→research→sub-component-becomes-project drift pattern.

name: feedback_skills_in_plans_are_tool_calls
purpose: Skill references inside plan documents (slash commands) are Skill tool invocations — never execute phases manually by reading SKILL.md.
notes: Session #273, T29 Wave 4 origin. Most detailed feedback file in this scope — contains extensive list of skills (analyze, synthesize, repo-analysis, document-analysis, media-analysis, deep-research, deep-plan, brainstorm, audit-*, debt-runner, todo, recall, gsd:*). Already in JASON-OS MEMORY.md. Some skill names (gsd:*, debt-runner, recall) are SoNash-specific but principle is portable.
portable_elements: Any /skillname in a plan is a Skill tool call, not prose instructions. Never manually perform skill phases. Let skill run its own orchestration.

name: feedback_stale_reviews_dist
purpose: SCHEMA_MAP test failures mean scripts/reviews/dist is stale — rebuild with npm run reviews:render (current command).
notes: CONTENT-BLEED (SIGNIFICANT): User-home says rebuild command is 'npm run reviews:render'. Canonical says 'npm run reviews:generate' (and description says 'rebuild with tsc or reviews:generate'). User-home explicitly notes 'reviews:generate script no longer exists (removed as of 2026-03-25)' and 'Current rebuild: npm run reviews:render'. Canonical is outdated. Not portable — SoNash-specific reviews build pipeline.
portable_elements: On test failures involving generated/compiled dist artifacts, rebuild before debugging code.

name: feedback_statusline_rebuild_safety
purpose: Statusline binary deployment: never overwrite running exe — use versioned filenames and git checkout to trigger reload.
sanitize_fields: Go version (1.26.1) and go.mod reference are SoNash-specific
notes: Session #244 (same as feedback_no_premature_next_steps). Requires Go toolchain. Binary deployment process is cross-project portable for any Claude Code statusline. Already in JASON-OS MEMORY.md. JASON-OS uses different binary name convention (jason-statusline-v2.exe).
portable_elements: Claude Code holds file handle to running exe — never overwrite. Versioned filename + git checkout is the safe deployment pattern. Project-level settings.json overrides user-level for statusLine.command.

name: feedback_sws_is_meta_plan
purpose: SWS is a meta-plan coordinating child plans — it does not absorb child plan content.
sanitize_fields: SWS name and child plan names (Tooling/CQ/DE) are SoNash-specific
notes: CONTENT-BLEED: user-home and canonical versions are substantively equivalent — no functional divergence detected. General principle: meta-plans coordinate child plans but do not absorb their content. Portable with sanitization of SoNash-specific plan names.
portable_elements: Meta-plan architecture: orchestrating plan references child plans via gates/sequences, does not absorb child plan content. Child plans remain their own execution docs.

name: feedback_tdms_intake_path
purpose: Canonical MASTER_DEBT.jsonl lives at docs/technical-debt/ — always use scripts/debt/intake-manual.js for intake, never write directly.
notes: 2026-04-04 origin (github-health triage). Contains full CLI template for intake-manual.js with all flags. Explicitly references reference_tdms_systems.md for related 'overwrite hazard'. SoNash-specific TDMS system — not portable to JASON-OS which has no TDMS.
portable_elements: Never write directly to canonical data stores — always use the designated intake/mutation scripts. Orphan files from wrong-path writes must be cleaned up and re-ingested.
